author: doll-agent
content: Implemented null-bitmap interop in ferray-numpy-interop::extras: dynarray_to_arrow_with_mask(arr, Option<&[bool]>) builds an Arrow PrimitiveArray with a validity buffer (mask[i]=true means null in Arrow's convention). arrow_to_dynarray_with_mask reads back (DynArray, Option<Vec<bool>>) — the mask is None when the Arrow array has no validity buffer, else a Vec<bool> with true=was-null. Null positions in the data buffer get T::default(); the mask carries the truth. array1_to_arrow_with_mask(arr, mask) is the typed-array convenience path that returns a properly-validity-buffered PrimitiveArray<T::ArrowType>. Vec<bool> primitive interface keeps ferray-numpy-interop free of a hard ferray-ma dependency — ma callers route through ma.mask().iter().copied().collect(). 7 unit tests cover round-trip, all-valid (mask=None), bool nulls, length-mismatch error, default-fill behavior, and the typed array1 path. Tracks #556.
